title: pterostylis scapula
intro: Pterostylis scapula, also known as the Muddy Shield Orchid, is a species of terrestrial orchid native to Australia. It is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant compounds. The health benefits of Pterostylis scapula include relief from fever, pain, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing and immune system support. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be used in topical applications such as creams and ointments.
